items.title: SLB Expands NVIDIA Partnership To Build AI Infrastructure For Energy
items.content: SLB (formerly Schlumberger) has significantly expanded its partnership with NVIDIA to develop advanced AI infrastructure specifically for the energy industry. This collaboration aims to accelerate digital transformation within the energy sector by leveraging AI and machine learning technologies. The initiative will likely focus on optimizing operations, improving efficiency, and driving innovation across various energy exploration and production processes.

items.title: SLB Industrializes AI for the Energy Industry with NVIDIA
items.content: SLB announced an expanded collaboration with NVIDIA to advance AI adoption in the energy industry. This partnership focuses on developing modular data center designs for NVIDIA DSX AI factories, creating an "AI Factory for Energy" with domain-specific generative AI models, and optimizing SLB's digital platforms for large datasets using NVIDIA's AI infrastructure. items.title: Four robots installed 100 MW of solar at one California site

This achievement demonstrates that intelligent field robotics can deliver sustained commercial production, exceeding one module per minute and significantly boosting regional productivity. items.title: Qualcomm Faces Challenges Amid Buyback Plan
items.content: Qualcomm is facing significant challenges, including a 25% stock price decline year-to-date due to a memory shortage impacting smartphone component sales. Despite these headwinds, the company announced a substantial $20 billion share buyback authorization and increased its quarterly dividend, signaling financial resilience. Qualcomm is also actively diversifying its revenue streams, with notable growth in its automotive and IoT segments.

items.title: Juniper Networks stock faces uncertainty after HPE acquisition integration amid AI networking boom
items.content: Juniper Networks stock, now part of Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) after its 2024 acquisition, is a key focus for US investors due to its AI-driven networking technology. HPE is integrating Juniper's Mist AI platform to challenge competitors in high-speed networking, capitalizing on the booming demand for AI infrastructure. The successful integration and Juniper's critical role in AI-optimized networks are expected to enhance HPE's margins and market position.

items.title: Intel Corporation stock faces critical juncture amid AI chip race and foundry challenges
items.content: Intel Corporation is at a critical juncture, navigating an intense AI chip race and its ambitious foundry expansion. The company is working to capture a larger share of the AI accelerator market with its Gaudi 3 chips and Xeon 6 processors, while simultaneously building out its Intel Foundry Services to compete with TSMC. Investors are closely watching Intel's execution on its chip roadmap and foundry strategy, supported by significant CHIPS Act funding.

items.title: Is It Time To Reassess Credo Technology Group Holding (CRDO) After Its Multi Year Surge?
items.content: Credo Technology Group Holding (CRDO) has experienced significant share price fluctuations, including a 103.9% gain over the past year, prompting a reassessment of its valuation. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock might be 11.9% overvalued, while a Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio comparison indicates it could be undervalued. The article highlights that different valuation methods and investor narratives can lead to varying fair value estimations for CRDO.

This framework addresses the security vulnerabilities inherent in these powerful AI agents, such as the skills supply chain, prompt injection, and self-evolving risks. Despite macro uncertainties, Akamai's strong position in edge security and its ability to layer AI-driven analytics position it as a defensive growth story for US investors.

items.title: Microsoft Options Spot-On: On March 24th, 829.42K Contracts Were Traded, With 3.45 Million Open Interest
items.content: On March 24th, 2026, Microsoft (MSFT) options trading saw significant activity with 829.42K contracts traded, and an open interest of 3.45 million contracts. This high level of trading volume and open interest indicates substantial investor attention and potential speculative moves around Microsoft's stock.

items.title: 美股周二三大指数全线收跌 软件股领跌赛富时跌超6% VCX因重仓Anthropic等AI初创暴涨64% Circle相关杠杆ETF剧烈分化 能源ETF领涨
items.content: On Tuesday, US stock indices broadly declined, with software stocks leading the downturn. However, the Fundrise Innovation Fund (VCX) surged by 64% due to its significant holdings in AI startups like Anthropic. Additionally, Circle-related leveraged ETFs experienced extreme volatility amidst regulatory concerns, while energy ETFs saw strong gains fueled by geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

items.title: ARM (NASDAQ:ARM) Stock Rating Upgraded by Raymond James Financial
items.content: Raymond James Financial has upgraded ARM (NASDAQ:ARM) from "market perform" to "outperform," setting a new price target of $166, which suggests a 23% upside. This upgrade follows ARM's announcement of its first Arm-designed data-center CPU, the AGI CPU, with Meta as the initial customer, and projections of $15 billion in annual revenue from this product line by the early 2030s. The article also details ARM's recent financial performance, institutional trading activity, and a summary of recent an

items.title: [Form 4] Meta Platforms, Inc. Insider Trading Activity
items.content: Meta Platforms, Inc. Chief Financial Officer Susan J. Li has reported receiving new equity awards, including stock options and restricted stock units (RSUs) tied to Class A Common Stock. The RSUs will vest quarterly over 16 installments starting May 15, 2026, subject to continued service. Stock options have specific per-share exercise prices and may vest earlier if Meta's share price meets certain levels by February 14, 2028, with remaining unvested options vesting through August 15, 2030.

items.title: Meta Platforms (NASDAQ: META) grants options to President Powell
items.content: Meta Platforms (NASDAQ: META) granted seven tranches of stock options to President and Vice Chairman Dina H. Powell on March 20, 2026, as compensation awards. These options have varying exercise prices and expire on March 19, 2031. Vesting conditions involve either the Class A share price meeting or exceeding the exercise price by February 14, 2028, or a time-based vesting schedule if price targets are not met.

items.title: Meta Platforms (META) awards stock option grants to Chief Legal Officer
items.content: Meta Platforms (META) Chief Legal Officer Curtis J. Mahoney received multiple grants of stock options on March 20, 2026. These options have varying exercise prices and expire on March 19, 2031, with vesting dependent on Meta's share price reaching specific thresholds or through a time-based schedule, conditioned on Mahoney's continued service. This Form 4 filing details the compensation-related grants and does not report any stock sales.
